response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong technical leadership capabilities and impressive breadth across multiple domains, they fall critically short on the role's core requirements. Most critically, the resume shows only 6 months of C++ experience at National Instruments versus the mandatory 8 years. Additionally, their career has been predominantly focused on web, product, and full-stack engineering rather than cloud platform infrastructure, distributed systems, and compute technologies that form the technical foundation of this role. The infrastructure experience present (microservices, Kubernetes, AWS CDK) is secondary and spans too few years to meet the 5-year large-scale infrastructure requirement.
strengths: Proven technical leadership across multiple Staff and Founder roles with demonstrated ability to mentor and establish engineering best practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality)
Exceptional technical breadth and adaptability across 15+ languages and domains (web, embedded, infrastructure, AI) showing ability to learn and master new technologies
Some relevant infrastructure exposure including microservices at scale, Kubernetes deployment, and AWS CDK work demonstrating familiarity with cloud and distributed systems concepts
concerns: Severe C++ experience gap: only 6 months documented versus mandatory 8-year requirement, representing a fundamental technical disqualification for the role
Career trajectory strongly emphasizes web, product, and full-stack development rather than cloud platform infrastructure and compute technologies that are central to this position
Insufficient evidence of cloud or DevOps engineering mentorship; leadership experience is in general software teams rather than specifically in cloud infrastructure or platform engineering contexts
